verdict_reasoning: TMED10 is a type I transmembrane p24-family cargo receptor whose dominant steady-state localization is the ER-Golgi intermediate compartment and Golgi cisternae, where it regulates COPI/COPII vesicular trafficking. However, multiple sources explicitly note it is also 'localized to the plasma membrane' as a cycling minority pool consistent with the constitutive Golgi-to-PM trafficking route of p24 proteins. Its luminal GOLD domain faces the extracellular-equivalent lumen at the PM, so the protein body reaches the outer face during this trafficking. There are no surface-directed therapeutic programs (ADC/CAR-T/bispecific). Classified as contextual via dual_localization: PM pool is real but minor relative to the dominant endomembrane compartment.
